differential_diagnoses: IgG4-related disease with systemic bone involvement
Erdheim-Chester disease (ECD) with IgG4+ plasma cell infiltration
Systemic lymphoproliferative disorder with pseudo-tumorous presentation
next_steps: Perform immunohistochemical staining for CD68 and CD1a on existing biopsy samples to evaluate for histiocytic disorders including ECD.
Perform BRAF V600E mutation analysis on the tibial biopsy specimen.
Measure serum IgG4, total IgG, and IgE levels.
Initiate systemic glucocorticoid therapy as an anti-inflammatory and diagnostic trial for suspected IgG4-RD.
Consult a hematopathology specialist to conduct an expert review of all previous biopsy samples.
Avoid unnecessary surgical excision of non-symptomatic bone lesions unless required for orthopedic stabilization.
Perform repeat serum protein electrophoresis and immunofixation to monitor the monoclonal band.
